perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient shall promptly return all copies of the Confidential Information except copies required for backup, disaster recovery, or business continuity and in such case the obligations hereunder shall survive until such copies are destroyed .
perturbation.changed_text: Within thirty (30) days after any written request by Sanmina, the Recipient may, at its discretion, return some or all copies of the Confidential Information, and the obligations hereunder shall immediately cease regardless of whether the copies are returned or destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original obligation to return *all* Confidential Information within ten days, and ensures the confidentiality survives if there are copies that are not destroyed. It now allows for a discretionary return within a longer timeframe (thirty days), and terminates confidentiality obligations even if not all copies are returned. This creates a conflict, as the agreement initially requires full return and continued confidentiality, whereas the altered term allows for partial return and immediate termination of confidentiality, creating uncertainty in the enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duty to Protect. Recipient shall protect the disclosed Confidential Informat ion by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, dissemination or publication of the Confidential Information as the Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient shall (i) 2 NDA Rev. 3 -14-14 Form NDA -3 (Supplier) limit access to all Confidential Information to its employees who shall reasonably require access to the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above, (ii) shall insure that all such employees have signed a nondisc losure agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement, (iii) shall use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the Purpose, and (iv) shall use due diligence to insure that all such persons comply with the provisions of this Agr eement. Recipient shall be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its employees or representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text mandates the return of *all* confidential information and maintains confidentiality until destruction. In contrast, the changed text allows for discretionary return of *some or all* copies, and immediately terminates confidentiality obligations regardless of destruction. This directly conflicts with the 'Duty to Protect' clause, which requires ongoing protection and limits access to confidential information. The change fundamentally undermines the obligation to protect confidential information as the original agreement intended, because the Recipient now has the discretion to not return all copies. This creates uncertainty and a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that the state courts of Santa Clara County, California and the federal courts located in the Northern District of the State of California shall have exclusive jurisdiction and venue to adjudicate any and all disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that any disputes arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitration shall take place in Los Angeles, California.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies exclusive jurisdiction and venue in Santa Clara County and the Northern District of California courts. The modified text mandates binding arbitration in Los Angeles. This is a direct contradiction concerning the dispute resolution mechanism and location, leading to uncertainty about how and where disputes will be resolved. This would be a contradiction against remedies section and duty to protect as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Recipient agrees that its obligations here under are necessary and reasonable in order to protect Sanmina and Sanmina’s business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages will be inadequate to compensate Sanmina for any breach by either party of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such viola tion or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to Sanmina and that, in addition to any other remedies th at may be avail able, in law, in equity or otherwise, Sanmina will be entitled to seek injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages and without the necessity of posting bond.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that disputes will be handled in Santa Clara County and the Northern District of California courts, while the changed text mandates binding arbitration in Los Angeles. The contradicted text discusses remedies in court (injunctive relief). The change from court jurisdiction to mandatory arbitration directly conflicts with the remedies section, which anticipates court proceedings.

perturbation.original_text: No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be o f any force unless in writing executed by all parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of any force unless in writing executed by an authorized representative of Sanmina. Oral agreements between the parties may also constitute amendments to this agreement, if documented via email correspondence.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires all modifications to be in writing and signed by all parties. The modification allows for amendments via written agreement from Sanmina only and by oral agreements documented in email correspondence, directly contradicting the original requirement for written agreements signed by all. This change could lead to disputes about the validity of oral or unilaterally signed modifications, creating significant uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement. This document contains the complete and exclusive Agreement between the parties, and it is intended to be final expression of their agreement. No promise, representation, warranty or covenant not included in this document has been or is relied upon by any party. Each party has relied upon its own examination of the warranties, representations and covenants expressly contained in the Agreement itself. No modification or amendment of this Agreement shall be of any force unless in writing executed by an authorized representative of Sanmina. Oral agreements between the parties may also constitute amendments to this agreement, if documented via email correspondence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows for modifications via oral agreements documented in email correspondence and written agreements from Sanmina only, which directly contradicts the 'Entire Agreement' clause that emphasizes the agreement's completeness and requires modifications to be in writing and executed by authorized representatives of ALL parties. The changed text introduces a clear and direct conflict within the document, meeting the definition of an inconsistency in-text contradiction. This contradicts the contradicted text.
